content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File                                          Date   : Feb. 15, 1977


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          Natalie West

Subject: Regulation 18404.

     When this regulation was drafted we considered the following
hypo:

          A committee (82013(c)) makes a contribution of
          $1,000 on January 1st, reports it in a post-
          election statement and terminates.

          If the committee makes $4,000 in additional
          contributions during the year, that commit-
          tee has again qualified. Aggregate all con-
          tributions made during the year.


NW:jo

sections.question_synthetic: How should contributions made by a committee under Political Code Section 82013(c) be aggregated for purposes of Regulation 18404, particularly when a committee makes an initial contribution, terminates, and then makes additional contributions later in the same year?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: When a committee makes a contribution, reports it in a post-election statement and terminates, but then makes additional contributions during the same year totaling $4,000 or more, the committee has again qualified and all contributions made during the year should be aggregated together.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting regulatory history and intent rather than a formal advice letter response to an external inquiry. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present. The document contains a hypothetical example (hypo) that illustrates the regulatory principle. Authored by Natalie West. Confidence reduced due to informal document structure and lack of traditional advice letter formatting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should contributions made by a committee under Political Code Section 82013(c) be aggregated for purposes of Regulation 18404, particularly when a committee makes an initial contribution, terminates, and then makes additional contributions later in the same year?

CONCLUSION: When a committee makes a contribution, reports it in a post-election statement and terminates, but then makes additional contributions during the same year totaling $4,000 or more, the committee has again qualified and all contributions made during the year should be aggregated together.

When this regulation was drafted we considered the following hypo: A committee (82013(c)) makes a contribution of $1,000 on January 1st, reports it in a post- election statement and terminates. If the committee makes $4,000 in additional contributions during the year, that commit- tee has again qualified. Aggregate all con- tributions made during the year. NW:jo
embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting the regulatory intent behind Regulation 18404 regarding the aggregation of contributions made by a committee under Political Code Section 82013(c) when the committee makes initial contributions, terminates, and then resumes contributions within the same calendar year.
